note: Eval prompts need user-supplied macOS screenshots as input files. Populate 'files' with real screenshot paths before running.

evals.name: first-digest-cold-corpus
evals.prompt: Digest these macOS app screenshots into my design corpus. The app is shown in the filenames.
evals.expected_output: Creates design-corpus/ with ledger.md (hash rows), apps/<app>.md with provenance-marked token tables, pattern entries for recognisable patterns, TASTE.md at Novice level with non-empty knowledge gaps, and per-screenshot digest blocks in chat with 14-point rubric scores. No canon promotions from a single app.

evals.name: icon-digest
evals.prompt: Study this mac app icon and add what you learn to the corpus.
evals.expected_output: Era classification, 12-point icon rubric with evidence, icons/<app>.md digest (palette ramps as hex, light model, layer stack, signature devices), ICONS.md created/updated, ledger row appended without disturbing existing rows.

evals.name: incremental-second-app-and-promotion-discipline
evals.prompt: Here are screenshots of a second app for the corpus. After digesting, tell me what the two apps have in common and whether anything is ready to become a rule.
evals.expected_output: Reads ledger first, skips nothing already digested, writes a second app profile, records shared observations as (recurring) in pattern files, explicitly declines canon promotion at 2 apps, and states what a third app would settle.

evals.name: design-mode-with-audit
evals.prompt: Using the corpus, design the main window mock (HTML/CSS) for a new macOS RSS reader aimed at the same audience as the digested apps.
evals.expected_output: States cluster choice + runner-up, inherits identity tokens with provenance, builds from pattern skeletons, self-audits against the 14-point rubric reporting an honest score, runs the lookalike check, and discloses corpus thinness if below Competent.
